skill.name: shadcn-aesthetic
skill.description: Guides AI coding tools to write modern CSS and SCSS using shadcn/ui-inspired design tokens, accessible states, dark mode, and consistent component patterns.
skill.summary: Generate refined shadcn-style CSS and SCSS for accessible, themeable web interfaces.
skill.author: Dieshen

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and network indicators. Review found the command, cryptography, and reconnaissance alerts are false positives from markdown code fences, CSS examples, and HSL color values; the only residual risk is low-risk external documentation and marketplace URLs.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static external command alerts point to markdown code fences and CSS or SCSS examples, not executable Ruby backticks or runtime shell execution. The files contain documentation for style generation and do not define scripts that run commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are fenced markdown examples or documentation blocks. No executable source file or command-dispatching code was found in the scanned files.

low_findings.title: CSS Color Values Misclassified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts are caused by CSS HSL numeric values, markdown metadata, or descriptive text such as destructive color tokens. No MD5, SHA-1, or other cryptographic API usage is present in the reviewed context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines are CSS theme tokens, version metadata, or file size text. There is no evidence of hashing, encryption, password storage, or signature verification logic.

low_findings.title: CSS Layout Examples Misclassified as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts correspond to CSS examples such as borders, focus rings, grid layouts, screen-reader utility styles, and visual comparison text. These examples do not collect host, network, process, or user information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is CSS styling guidance, not system or network inspection. No calls to operating system, network, or browser reconnaissance APIs were found.

low_findings.title: External Documentation and Marketplace URLs
low_findings.description: The skill includes hardcoded URLs for marketplace installation, project documentation, shadcn/ui, Radix UI, Tailwind CSS, and author links. These are user-directed references and do not automatically exfiltrate data, but they create a normal community-source supply-chain consideration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are directly visible in documentation and metadata, so the finding is real. Risk is low because the skill contains no automatic network request code.

content.value_statement: Modern interface work often drifts into inconsistent CSS. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward shadcn-style tokens, spacing, accessibility, and dark mode.
actual_capabilities: Provides HSL CSS variable systems for colors and themes.
Defines spacing, typography, radius, shadow, and z-index scales.
Shows component patterns for buttons, inputs, cards, badges, and alerts.
Includes focus-visible, screen-reader, high contrast, and reduced motion patterns.
Demonstrates before-and-after refactors and a CSS quality checklist.
limitations: Does not include executable generators, linters, or build tooling.
Focuses on CSS and SCSS guidance, not application component logic.
Shadcn-inspired values may need brand and product adjustments.
External links are references and are not fetched automatically by the skill.

use_cases.title: Standardize a Web Design System
use_cases.description: Create shared tokens and component styles that keep multiple pages visually consistent.

use_cases.title: Refactor Legacy CSS
use_cases.description: Replace hard-coded colors, random spacing, and weak focus states with reusable modern patterns.

use_cases.title: Prototype Accessible UI Components
use_cases.description: Generate buttons, inputs, cards, and alerts with dark mode and keyboard-visible states.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Styled Button
prompt_templates.prompt: Create CSS for a primary button using shadcn-style tokens, accessible focus states, hover states, and dark mode support.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Existing Styles
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor my existing CSS to use HSL variables, a 4px spacing scale, subtle shadows, and focus-visible states.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Component Set
prompt_templates.prompt: Design SCSS patterns for buttons, inputs, cards, badges, and alerts that share one token system.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a UI Theme
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this theme for shadcn-style consistency, accessibility gaps, dark mode readiness, and maintainable token usage.

output_examples.input: A request to modernize an old button style.
output_examples.output: A refined button approach using semantic color tokens, consistent padding, subtle elevation, hover feedback, and keyboard-visible focus treatment.

output_examples.input: A request for a reusable card and form style system.
output_examples.output: A compact design system plan covering surface colors, borders, radius, spacing, input states, and dark mode behavior.

output_examples.input: A request to check CSS quality before release.
output_examples.output: A review checklist covering variable usage, contrast, reduced motion, focus states, spacing consistency, and hard-coded value removal.

best_practices: Start with shared CSS variables before styling individual components.
Use focus-visible states and reduced motion support for interactive elements.
Keep spacing, shadows, typography, and radius values on consistent scales.
anti_patterns: Mixing hard-coded colors with token-based theme values.
Removing browser outlines without adding an accessible focus replacement.
Using random pixel values that break visual rhythm across components.

faq.question: Does this skill install shadcn/ui?
faq.answer: No. It provides CSS and SCSS guidance inspired by shadcn/ui design patterns.

faq.question: Can it work with React, Vue, or Blazor?
faq.answer: Yes. The patterns are CSS-focused and can be adapted to common web frameworks.

faq.question: Does it generate Tailwind classes?
faq.answer: It focuses on CSS and SCSS architecture, but the design principles align with Tailwind-based systems.

faq.question: Does it support dark mode?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance uses CSS variables that can switch values under a dark class.

faq.question: Is the skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found no executable malware behavior, with only low-risk external reference links.

faq.question: What should users review before adoption?
faq.answer: Users should review external marketplace and documentation links because this is a community-source skill.
